headline: Urban Heat Island Makes KL 4.2 Degrees Hotter Than Rural
context: MetMalaysia data shows central KL consistently 4.2 degrees Celsius warmer than surrounding rural areas due to heat island effect.

cards.big: KL city centre is 4.2 degrees hotter than surrounding rural areas
cards.sub: Urban heat island intensified 40% since 2010 measurements began

cards.big: Green cover in KL declined from 42% to 28% between 2000 and 2025
cards.sub: Tree canopy loss of 14,000 hectares removed natural cooling capacity

cards.big: Electricity for cooling in KL buildings costs RM4.8 billion annually
cards.sub: Each 1 degree increase raises cooling energy demand by 6%

cards.big: DBKL tree planting aims for 100,000 trees but planted only 28,000 since 2022
cards.sub: Survival rate of 55% due to poor soil and maintenance gaps

cards.big: Heat islands test whether urban planning accounts for temperature consequences
KL is 4.2 degrees hotter than surrounding rural areas. Urban heat demands green infrastructure, not just buildings.
